rulings.Case Title: GEORGE LUCAS,JR et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Defendant Gencorp, Inc. To Produce Its Custodian Of Records And Person Most Knowledgeable For Deposition And To Produce Documents At Deposition
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Wednesday, September 21, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:00 a.m., Line 1. Plaintiffs¿ motion to compel defendant GenCorp, Inc. to produce its custodian of records and person most knowledgeable for deposition and to produce documents at deposition was continued on the court¿s own motion to give the parties additional time to meet and confer. If all issues raised by this motion have not been resolved before then, by 4:30pm on September 20 the parties must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org stating, without argument, which issues remain in dispute and then appear at the hearing on September 21. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: MARY ROBERTA HOLLAND VS. A.W. CHESTERTON COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Fraser'S Boiler Service, Inc.'S Motion To Compel Further Deposition Of Plaintiff Christopher Holland
rulings.Rulings: On Discovery Calendar for Wednesday, September 21, 2011, line 1. Defendant Fraser¿s Boiler Service¿s motion to compel further deposition of plaintiff Christopher Holland will be heard at 9:30am in Dept. 503, Hon. Teri L. Jackson presiding. Defendant¿s motion is granted. Defendant demonstrated good cause to allow it to take a subsequent deposition of plaintiff pursuant to C.C.P. 2025.610(b). The subsequent deposition only applies to defendant Fraser¿s Boiler Service. Other defendants may attend the deposition but may not ask questions. Defendant to bear all costs associated with the further deposition. Prevailing party to bring a proposed order to the hearing. If plaintiff properly notifies the court that it is going to appear to contest this tentative ruling, plaintiff must bring a proposed order to the hearing that would conform to a ruling in plaintiff¿s favor. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: ANDREW JUSTICE et al VS. AMCHEM PRODUCTS, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Costs And Fees
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Wednesday, September 21, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:00 a.m., Line 3. Plaintiffs¿ motion for costs and fees against defendant Merlex Stucco, Inc. is denied. Plaintiff failed to demonstrate that Merlex Stucco¿s motion to quash plaintiffs¿ deposition notice and for a protective order was frivolous or solely intended to cause unnecessary delay. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Calendar Matter: Joinder In Plaintiffs' Motion To Compel Benjamin Moore Company'S Responjses To Response To General Order No. 129 Standard Plaintiffs' Interrogatories To Defendants And Request For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Wednesday, September 21, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:00 a.m., Line 4. Waters, Kraus & Paul¿s joinder in Keller, Fishback & Jackson¿s motion to compel Benjamin Moore Co.¿s responses to General Order 129 standard interrogatories to defendants and request for monetary sanctions is off calendar as moot. Defendant served verified responses on September 7. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Benjamin Moore Company¿S Responses To General Order No. 129 Standard Interrogatories To Defendants And Request For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Wednesday, September 21, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:00 a.m., Line 4. Keller, Fishback & Jackson¿s motion to compel Benjamin Moore Co.¿s responses to General Order 129 standard interrogatories to defendants and request for monetary sanctions is off calendar as moot. Defendant served verified responses on September 7. All requests for sanctions are denied. Sanctions would not serve any useful purpose at this point. = (220/HEK)
